title: Evaluation of New Zealand's future census options for 2028 and beyond: a report prepared by the Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel  | Stats NZ
content:
## Evaluation of New Zealand's future census options for 2028 and beyond: a report prepared by the Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel
18 June 2025, 10:15am
Recommendations from the Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel evaluating how future censuses could be delivered.
The Government Statistician convened the Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel in July 2024 to provide an impartial and robust review of possible approaches for modernising the census.
The Panel's report includes 83 recommendations and played a key role in informing the Government Statistician's in-principle decision on how the next census will be run, and Stats NZ's work to realise it.
- Evaluation of New Zealands future census options for 2028 and beyond Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 1.1 MB

### Evaluation of New Zealands future census options for 2028 and beyond
Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 1.1 MB

## Response to the Future Census Independent Evaluation Panel report
This response identifies the Panel recommendations accepted by Stats NZ for actioning, and the recommendations that we commit to considering during the design phases for the next census.

## Download the response to the report
